pi_items.source: Queried the RCSB PDB API and verified 1TSR corresponds to the 'p53 core domain in complex with DNA' at 2.2 Angstrom resolution (Cho et al., 1994).
Inspected export_result.sh which hardcodes saving the evaluated state dictionary to this exact JSON file path.
Inspected setup_task.sh and export_result.sh, both of which explicitly reference and operate on this file path for image creation checks.
Inspected setup_task.sh and export_result.sh, both of which explicitly reference and operate on this file path for text report generation checks.
Inspected verifier.py logic where this exact minimum threshold is enforced for full credit on the image validation step.

Verified through a web search of scientific literature that these six amino acid positions are indeed the predominant cancer-associated hotspot mutations in the p53 DNA-binding domain. verifier.py also specifically scans for these integers.
Inspected verifier.py logic which requires identifying at least 4 out of the 6 hotspot residues for maximum credit on this scoring criterion.

Wrote a python script to parse 1TSR.pdb and calculate the actual distances from ARG 248 and ARG 273 to the DNA chains (2.27 A and 2.53 A, respectively), verifying that a 2.0 A lower bound is structurally sound. Checked verifier.py logic enforcing this.
Inspected verifier.py logic that filters parsed numeric distances and ensures they fall under this upper bound, bounding plausible contact distances.
Inspected verifier.py logic where full credit for the report length requires >= 8 lines.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires generating a biological visualization and report based on PDB entry 1TSR, which was independently confirmed to be the p53 core domain in complex with DNA (Cho et al., 1994). The six specified hotspots (175, 245, 248, 249, 273, 282) are verified against the scientific literature as the primary cancer-associated mutations in p53. By writing a script to calculate distances within the actual 1TSR.pdb structure, the minimum contact distances from ARG 248 and ARG 273 to the DNA were found to be approximately 2.27 Å and 2.53 Å respectively, confirming that the verifier's acceptable distance bounds of 2.0 to 10.0 Å are structurally correct and physically sound.
